title: As Trump Pushes Deportations, a Skyrocketing Caseload Strains Immigration Courts
summary: A federal surge has more than doubled caseloads within some immigration courts nationwide. Lawyers say the tactic is causing errors and confusion.

title: Paxton’s Senate Bid Raises the Stakes in His War on Latino Voting Groups
summary: The Texas attorney general has mounted an all-out effort to prove Democratic Hispanic groups have been corrupting elections. Now he could be the beneficiary of his own attacks.

title: ICE Says Detainees Are ‘Worst of the Worst.’ Government Data Disagrees.
summary: Federal officials said they are removing killers and rapists from the streets. Data obtained by The New York Times indicates most detainees at a Newark facility haven’t been convicted of crimes.

title: Newark Mayor to Scale Back Police Presence at Delaney Hall ICE Detention Center
summary: The mayor, Ras Baraka, said the city would not spend taxpayer money to safeguard Delaney Hall, an immigration detention center in Newark run by a private prison company.

title: Xavier Becerra Advances in California Governor Race
summary: Mr. Becerra was long dismissed in the contest until the abrupt departure of Eric Swalwell created a surprise path for an experienced Democrat.

title: Amid Mounting Democratic Concern, Platner Says His Past Is Being ‘Weaponized’
summary: The presumptive Democratic Senate nominee in Maine said the state would have his back in the face of accusations he has denied.

title: Deluged With Mail Ballots, California Takes Its Time Counting Votes Again
summary: Experts say speeding up the count in California would take more resources, but also scaling back rules that expand voting access.

title: What Are Democrats’ Best Senate Pickup Opportunities?
summary: We’ve grouped six battleground states by some of the factors that shape how likely it is for the party to flip each Senate seat.

title: What Visual Evidence Tells Us About Israel’s Use of White Phosphorus in Lebanon
summary: Videos collected by The Times shows how the Israeli military has deployed a munition that can be extremely harmful over populated areas in Lebanon.

title: Iran Fired Drones Toward Strait of Hormuz With U.S. Shooting Down at Least 4, U.S. Military Says
summary: The latest violence between the United States and Iran threatened a cease-fire and risked further stoking tensions in the region.

title: For Dubai’s Migrants, War Is One More Worry in Super Stressed Lives
summary: For laborers in Dubai, a free stress-management class offers a temporary oasis of calm amid struggles with debt, loneliness, long hours and, in recent months, the fear of missile strikes.

title: Trump Greets Farmers in Wisconsin, but Says He Could Be Home Watching TV
summary: President Trump was in Wisconsin to reassure farmers who have been stung by his tariff policies and rising fuel prices from the war in Iran.

title: Trump Administration Investigating Gender Treatments at Mount Sinai
summary: A grand jury subpoenaed the health system for information about adolescent patients who had received gender-related care, Mount Sinai said on Friday.

title: Trump Says Bill Pulte, His New Intelligence Director, Should Slash Staff
summary: The president suggested that employees who worked for previous Democratic presidents were among those who should be fired.

title: Trump Pardons Former Congressman Convicted of Insider Trading
summary: Stephen Buyer, a former Republican representative from Indiana, was convicted of trading stock related to two deals before they were made public.

title: As Ebola Spreads in East Africa, Will China Step Up?
summary: China is well positioned to help stop the deadly virus, and could move into a gap left by U.S. retreat.

title: The World Has Learned From the Last Ebola Outbreak, but Gaps Remain
summary: The vaccine development process and coordination between health organizations have improved since a devastating 2014-2016 outbreak.

title: Raphael Warnock Says the Supreme Court Has Done ‘Violence’ to Democracy
summary: The Democrat from Georgia on what he sees as the moral issues of our time.

title: Before French Open Finals, They’ll Serve Up a Dance
summary: The choreographer Benjamin Millepied had an unusual brief: To create two short works for the championship matches at Roland Garros.

title: Norma Jeane’s Still Got It!
summary: Some continue to like it hot.

title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
summary: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

title: Balancing Act
summary: The tension between sticking to what we know and seeking out new experiences is one of comfort versus novelty. How can we have both?

title: ‘60 Minutes’ Is a ‘Cage Full of Tigers.’ Can Nick Bilton Lead It?
summary: The tech journalist and filmmaker was a surprise choice to lead a famously combative newsroom. His first week was chaos.
